originalTitle: Goodbye officer, hello touchscreen - a vision for policing in Ethiopia
title: Super Screens to Help Keep Ethiopia Safe!
content:
Jambo, my amazing friends across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter, here to tell you something super exciting happening in the beautiful country of Ethiopia! Get ready to hear about new helpers that are coming to our towns and cities. They are not people, but they are very smart!

New Friends: The Smart Screens!

Imagine going to a special booth, kind of like a small house, in your neighborhood. And guess what? Inside, there isn't a person always sitting there, but there is a super cool screen! You can touch this screen, and it can talk to you. It's like a giant tablet or a computer helper! These special spots are called "unmanned sites." "Unmanned" just means there isn't a person always there. But don't worry, these screens are very friendly and ready to help! They are part of a big new plan to make things easier and safer for everyone in Ethiopia. Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in the world, with so much history and wonderful people!

What Can These Smart Screens Do?

These new screen helpers are like your own quick link to help. If you ever get lost, or if you see something that makes you worried, you can go to one of these screens. You can touch it, and it will help you tell someone what happened. Maybe your favorite toy went missing, or you saw a lost puppy. You can go to the screen and quickly tell the police! The screen will help you talk to a real police officer who is in another place. It's like a video call, but super easy for everyone to use. It's a fast way to get information too! Maybe you want to know how to stay safe near the road, or what to do if you find something important. The screen can help you find answers to these questions. It’s a bit like having a helpful talking book right there!

Why Are These Screens Special?

These screens are special because they can be open almost all the time! Even if it's late at night or very early in the morning, the screen will be ready to help. This means help is always just a few steps away, no matter the time. Sometimes, police officers are very busy helping with big problems. These screens can help with smaller things quickly. This lets our wonderful police officers focus on important jobs that need them to be there in person. It helps everyone work smarter! Think about it: these screens can be put in many different places. This means more people, even in small villages or far-away towns, can get help quickly. It helps everyone feel connected and safe. It's like having a friendly helper in every community. Did you know? Police officers are special helpers in our communities who work hard to keep everyone safe and sound!

Digital Magic for Ethiopia!

This big project uses something called "digital technologies." It sounds like a big word, but it just means using smart tools like computers, screens, and the internet to make our lives better and easier. Just like how you might use a tablet to learn at school, Ethiopia is using smart tech to help police and communities. It's like magic, but it's really clever science! These digital tools help police officers do their jobs even better. They can share information faster and find solutions quicker. This makes our communities in Ethiopia even safer places to live and play. Ethiopia is becoming super modern and smart! They are using new ideas to solve problems and make sure everyone feels looked after. It shows that Ethiopia is growing and ready for the future, full of exciting new ways to do things. Did you know? "Digital technology" just means using computers and smart devices to do amazing things, like helping people talk to each other!

A Safer, Happier Tomorrow!

These new screen helpers are just the start of many cool things coming to Ethiopia. They show how much Ethiopia cares about keeping everyone safe and happy, especially kids like you! Soon, you might see these friendly screens helping people all over the country. They are there to make reporting things easy and getting help simple. It's an exciting time for Ethiopia, bringing new smart ways to keep our communities strong and safe. So, next time you hear about a smart screen helper, you'll know all about this wonderful new way Ethiopia is looking after its people. It's a big step towards a brighter, safer future for everyone! Keep exploring and learning, my friends!
summary: Ethiopia is introducing new smart screens and digital tools in different places to help people quickly report things and get information from the police, making communities safer and more connected.
whyMatters: This is important because it means everyone, even kids, can get help quickly from police, no matter where they are or what time it is, helping keep our communities in Ethiopia safer and smarter for all.

title: Belgium detains three suspected Cameroon separatists in war crimes probe
content: Money is reportedly being raised by them for the armed struggle, prosecutors say.

originalTitle: US sanctions Rwanda's military and top commanders over fighting in DR Congo
title: A Big 'Time-Out' for Friends: News from Africa!
content:
Hello, my super friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter, here to tell you a story about some grown-up stuff, but in a way that's easy to understand. We're going to talk about two countries in Africa, Rwanda and DR Congo, and a big country far away called the USA.

Let's Meet Our Friends!

Imagine our world as a big playground. We have many, many countries, just like we have many friends on the playground. Today, we're looking at a part of Africa where two countries live close to each other. One country is called Rwanda. It's a beautiful place, sometimes called the 'Land of a Thousand Hills' because it has so many green hills. It's famous for its amazing mountain gorillas! **Did you know?** Mountain gorillas are very special animals, and they live in the misty mountains of countries like Rwanda and DR Congo. Next to Rwanda is a much bigger country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's huge, with big forests and lots of different animals and people. And far, far away across the big ocean, is a country called the United States of America, or the USA. The USA is a very big country too, and sometimes it tries to help other countries be peaceful.

What's the News About?

Sometimes, even grown-up countries can have disagreements. And sometimes, these disagreements can lead to what we call 'fighting'. Fighting means that people from different groups are not getting along, and sometimes soldiers are involved. In our story, there has been some fighting happening in DR Congo. It makes people sad when there is fighting, because it can make people leave their homes, and schools might close. Everyone wants peace, where people can play and learn safely.

What is a 'Military'?

Every country has a group of brave men and women called the 'military'. Their job is to protect their country and keep everyone safe. They wear uniforms and train very hard. In the military, there are also 'commanders'. Commanders are like the teachers or leaders of the military. They tell the soldiers what to do and help them stay organized. Our news story talks about the military of Rwanda and its top commanders.

What is a 'Sanction'? (A Big 'Time-Out'!)

Now, here's the tricky part: what does 'sanctions' mean? Imagine you and your friend are playing. If your friend does something that makes you sad or breaks a rule, maybe you say, "I'm not going to share my toys with you for a little while," or "I'm not going to help you build your sandcastle today." This is a bit like what 'sanctions' are for countries. The USA felt that Rwanda's military was involved in the fighting in DR Congo. So, the USA decided to give Rwanda's military a kind of 'time-out'. These 'time-outs' for countries mean that the USA might stop doing helpful things for Rwanda's military. They might stop sharing money, or stop letting them buy certain things. It's a way for the USA to say, "We are not happy about the fighting, and we want it to stop." **Did you know?** Countries often talk to each other to try and solve problems, just like you might talk to a friend if you have a disagreement.

Rwanda Says, 'That's Not Fair!'

But Rwanda's government, the people who run the country, said they were not happy with this 'time-out' from the USA. They said it was "one-sided" and "politically motivated." What does "one-sided" mean? Imagine two children are arguing. If a grown-up only listens to one child's story and decides who is wrong without hearing the other child, that's "one-sided." Rwanda felt the USA only listened to one side of the story about the fighting in DR Congo. And "politically motivated"? This means Rwanda felt the USA made this decision because of their government's ideas and not just because of what happened on the ground. They felt it was unfair and had other reasons behind it. **Did you know?** When countries make decisions, they often think about what is best for their own people and what they believe is right, just like you think about what's best for you and your friends.

Why is This Important?

This news reminds us that grown-ups and countries sometimes have big problems to solve. It's important for everyone to try and be peaceful and listen to each other. When there is fighting, it can hurt many innocent people, especially children. We all hope that countries can find a way to talk and play nicely together, just like we do on our playground. Peace means everyone can go to school, play outside, and be happy and safe in their homes. And that's what we all wish for! **Did you know?** The United Nations is a very big club of almost all the countries in the world, and they work together to try and keep peace and help people everywhere!
summary: The USA has placed sanctions (like a 'time-out') on Rwanda's military because of fighting in DR Congo, but Rwanda says the decision is unfair and only looks at one side of the story.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries fight, many people, especially children, can get hurt or lose their homes, and we all want peace so everyone can be safe and happy.

title: Russia, China raise diplomatic voices against US-Israeli attacks on Iran
content: China's foreign minister tells Israel to end attacks; Russian FM Lavrov says no sign Tehran seeking nuclear bomb.

originalTitle: US Commerce Secretary Lutnick to testify before Congress about Epstein ties
title: Our Young Green Heroes! Kids Helping Nature in Africa!
content:
Hello, hello, wonderful friends across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some super exciting news from our amazing continent. We have a story about children who are making a big difference right in their own neighborhoods!

Meet Our Green Heroes!

In a sunny village called Harambee, which means 'pulling together' in Swahili, some very special kids are busy. They are called 'Green Heroes'! These children are learning all about nature and helping to keep our Earth healthy and happy. They are just like you, maybe in Class 2 or a little older! They go to Harambee Primary School, and their teacher, Miss Amina, helps them learn about plants and animals. But they don't just learn in the classroom. Oh no, they go outside and get their hands a little bit dirty – in a good way! They are planting and caring for our world.

Planting Tiny Trees, Growing Big Hope

One of the most important things the Green Heroes do is plant trees. They have tiny tree seeds and little baby trees, called saplings. They dig small holes in the ground and carefully put the saplings in. Then they give them water and make sure they have enough sunshine. Planting trees is like planting hope for the future. Every tree they plant helps our world in so many wonderful ways. It's a big job for little hands!

Simple Explanation: A sapling is like a baby tree. It's very small and needs lots of care to grow big and strong, just like a baby animal or a young child. It can't stand on its own yet without help.

Did you know? One big tree can give enough oxygen for four people to breathe for a whole day! That's why planting trees helps us all.

Why Are Trees So Important?

Trees are super important friends to us and to animals. They make the air we breathe clean and fresh. Imagine them as big, natural air filters! They suck up the dirty air and blow out clean air for us to enjoy. Trees also give homes to many animals, like birds, monkeys, and tiny insects. These animals need trees to live and find food. Trees provide shade when the sun is very hot, making it cooler for us to play outside and for animals to rest. They also help to keep the soil strong so it doesn't wash away when it rains a lot. This stops our beautiful land from getting damaged.

Helping Our Animal Friends

The Green Heroes also learn about the animals that live near their village. They learn how to protect them and make sure their homes are safe. They understand that every animal, big or small, has an important job in nature, like bees helping flowers or birds eating bugs. Sometimes, they help clean up rubbish from areas where animals live. Rubbish can be very dangerous for animals. A plastic bag might look like food to a hungry animal, but it can make them very sick or even hurt them badly. So keeping places clean is a big help for our animal friends.

Simple Explanation: Rubbish means trash or waste, like empty plastic bottles, old food wrappers, or broken toys. It's important to put it in bins so it doesn't harm nature or animals. It keeps our planet tidy.

Did you know? Africa is home to many amazing animals, like elephants, giraffes, zebras, and lions! Protecting their homes, called habitats, helps them stay safe and happy for many years.

Learning and Fun Go Together!

Miss Amina makes learning about nature really fun. They sing songs about trees and animals. They draw colourful pictures of what a healthy forest looks like. They even go on exciting nature walks to find different kinds of leaves and flowers. It's like a big adventure! They learn the names of local plants and birds. It's like a big treasure hunt every time they go outside! And the best part is, they are doing something good for everyone while having fun and making new friends. Learning outside is so much fun!

Spreading the Green Message

The Green Heroes are not just keeping the good work to themselves. They are telling their families and friends about what they are doing. They explain why it’s important to save water, not to litter, and to plant more trees. They share their green secrets! Their enthusiasm is catching on! Other children in nearby villages are hearing about the Green Heroes and asking how they can start their own groups. Soon, there might be Green Heroes all over Africa, making our whole continent greener and brighter!

Did you know? Bees help plants grow by carrying pollen from flower to flower. This helps us get fruits and vegetables! They are tiny but very important Green Heroes too!

How Can YOU Be a Green Hero?

You might be thinking, “How can I be a Green Hero too?” Well, it’s easier than you think! You don’t need to plant a whole forest by yourself. Every little action helps, just like a single drop of water helps a plant grow. Here are some simple ways you can be a Green Hero every day: * You can save water by turning off the tap when you brush your teeth or when soaping your hands. * You can make sure your rubbish goes into a dustbin and not on the ground. * You can help your parents water plants at home or in your garden. * You can learn about the animals and plants that live near you – maybe read a book or watch a show! * You can tell your friends and family about why it's good to protect nature and keep our Earth clean. Even small steps can make a big, big difference. Imagine if every child in Africa did just one green thing every day! Our continent would be even more beautiful, healthy, and full of happy creatures.

Simple Explanation: Littering means dropping rubbish on the ground instead of putting it in a bin. It makes places dirty, can harm animals if they eat it, and makes our beautiful world look messy. So, always find a bin!

Did you know? Recycling old things like plastic bottles, paper, or metal helps save trees and energy! It means we don't have to make new things from scratch every time.

A Bright Future for Africa

The Green Heroes of Harambee village are showing us all a wonderful path. They remind us that even young children have the power to protect our planet. They are growing trees, protecting animals, and inspiring everyone around them to care for nature. Thank you, Green Heroes, for being such shining examples! Keep up the amazing work. And to all of you watching and reading, remember: you can be a Green Hero too! Let's all work together to keep Africa green and full of life!
summary: Children in Harambee village, called 'Green Heroes', are planting trees, protecting animals, and learning about nature to help keep Africa beautiful and healthy for everyone.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows how even young children can make a big positive difference in protecting our Earth, making the air cleaner, giving homes to animals, and inspiring everyone to care for our shared natural world.
